title: Add Mock IP Database with Search, Filter, and Backend Persistence

requirements.text: Add a new 'IP Database' page (or expand the existing IPDatabase page) that pre-loads a set of at least 20 mock IP records on the frontend with the following fields: IP address, owner/organization, country, city, registration date, and status (active/flagged/blocked). These records should be hardcoded as frontend sample data and do not need to be stored in the backend.

quotes: mock data should just be front end
Pre-loaded sample data on the frontend with fields: IP address, owner/organization, country, city, registration date, and status (active/flagged/blocked)

acceptanceCriteria: At least 20 mock IP records are visible on the IP Database page on first load
Each record displays: IP address, owner/organization, country, city, registration date, and status badge
Mock data is defined in frontend source only (no backend calls for initial data)

requirements.text: Implement search and lookup functionality on the IP Database page allowing users to search for IP records by IP address. Results should filter in real-time or on submit.
acceptanceCriteria: A search input is present on the IP Database page
Typing an IP address or partial IP filters the displayed records
Search works across both mock frontend data and any backend-persisted entries

requirements.text: Add filter controls on the IP Database page to filter records by status (active/flagged/blocked), country, and at minimum one additional field (e.g., organization). Filters should be combinable.

quotes: Browse & filter by status, country, and more

acceptanceCriteria: Dropdown or toggle filters for status and country are present
Selecting a filter narrows the displayed records accordingly
Multiple filters can be applied simultaneously
A clear/reset filters option is available

requirements.text: Add a backend Motoko data structure and CRUD endpoints to store IP database records with fields: IP address, owner/organization, country, city, registration date, and status. Expose functions to add, update, delete, and query these records.

quotes: any added IP should be added to the backend in the canisters
Add new IPs — persisted to the Motoko backend canister
Edit & delete entries from both frontend and backend

acceptanceCriteria: Backend actor exposes addIPRecord, updateIPRecord, deleteIPRecord, and listIPRecords (or getIPRecord) functions
IP records are stored in stable memory and survive canister upgrades
Each record has a unique ID assigned by the backend

requirements.text: Add an 'Add IP' form/modal on the IP Database page that allows users to submit a new IP record (IP address, owner/organization, country, city, registration date, status). On submission, the new record must be persisted to the Motoko backend canister via the addIPRecord endpoint and immediately reflected in the displayed list.

quotes: Add new IPs — persisted to the Motoko backend canister
any added IP should be added to the backend in the canisters

acceptanceCriteria: A button opens an 'Add IP Record' form/modal with all required fields
On successful submission, the record is saved to the backend canister
The new record appears in the IP Database list without a full page reload
Form validates required fields before submission
IP address format is validated (IPv4/IPv6)

requirements.text: Add edit functionality to IP Database records. Clicking 'Edit' on a record (both mock and backend-persisted entries) opens a pre-filled form/modal. On save, backend-persisted records are updated via the updateIPRecord endpoint; mock-only records are updated locally in frontend state.

quotes: Edit & delete entries from both frontend and backend

acceptanceCriteria: Each record row/card has an Edit button
Clicking Edit opens a pre-filled form/modal
Saving a backend record calls updateIPRecord and refreshes the list
Editing a frontend-only mock record updates it in local state

requirements.text: Add delete functionality to IP Database records. Clicking 'Delete' on a record removes it. For backend-persisted records, call the deleteIPRecord endpoint; for frontend-only mock records, remove from local state. Show a confirmation prompt before deletion.

quotes: Edit & delete entries from both frontend and backend

acceptanceCriteria: Each record row/card has a Delete button
A confirmation dialog is shown before deletion
Deleting a backend record calls deleteIPRecord and removes it from the list
Deleting a mock-only record removes it from local state immediately

requirements.text: Wire the IP Database page to load backend-persisted records (from listIPRecords) on mount and merge them with the frontend mock data for display. Use React Query for fetching and caching backend records, consistent with the existing useQueries.ts pattern.
acceptanceCriteria: On page load, backend-persisted IP records are fetched and displayed alongside mock data
React Query is used for fetching backend records (consistent with useQueries.ts)
Loading and error states are handled gracefully in the UI

constraints: All Motoko logic must remain in backend/main.mo (single-actor architecture)
Do not modify files under frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, or frontend/src/components/ui
Mock/sample data must be defined only in the frontend — it must not be seeded into the backend canister
Maintain the existing gold-and-black dark theme and Tailwind CSS styling conventions
nonGoals: Seeding or migrating mock frontend data into the Motoko backend
IP geolocation API integration (all location data is manual/mock)
Authentication requirements for browsing or searching the IP database
Pagination beyond basic display of all records
